political.s: FEC: $252K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 14 executive donors.  [CA CalAccess + NY COELIG + TX Ethics adds state-level totals.]

charity.s: $165M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.36% of revenue) via Coca-Cola Foundation. Drucker Institute Management Top 250 — ranked #13 (2024) on customer satisfaction, employee engagement, innovation, social responsibility, and financial strength. Fortune World's Most Admired Companies — ranked #19 (2024). Newsweek Most Trustworthy Companies in America — ranked #5 (2024). Operates the The Coca-Cola Foundation Inc: $95.9M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2023, IRS Form 990-PF).
charity.focus: Water stewardship, watershed conservation, recycling value chains, disaster preparedness, education, economic prosperity

environment.s: environmental violation: $385K in federal penalties (Violation Tracker).

labor.s: 123 federal penalties totaling $265.8M since 2000 (Violation Tracker). Supply-chain signals: KnowTheChain 58/100; CHRB 13.8/26; TVPRA exposure; UK MS statement; AU MS statement.

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

animals.s: Ethical Consumer Avoid — environment + plastic-pollution + tax + political-donations concerns. [ethical-consumer]

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 739:1 (median employee pay $17,947) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.parent: Berkshire Hathaway, The Vanguard Group, BlackRock, Berkshire Hathaway, The Vanguard Group, BlackRock, State Street Corporation, Fidelity Investments, Morgan Stanley, Geode Capital Management, Charles Schwab Corporation, J.P. Morgan & Co., Norges Bank
wiki.extract: The Coca-Cola Company is an American multinational corporation founded in January 1892, headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia. It manufactures, sells and markets soft drinks including Coca-Cola, other non-alcoholic beverage concentrates and syrups, and alcoholic beverages. Its stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange and is a component of the DJIA and the S&P 500 and S&P 100 indices.

governance.s: Wikipedia "Criticism" section: Criticism of Coca-Cola has arisen from various groups around the world, concerning a variety of issues, including health effects, environmental issues, and business practices. The drink's coca…

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by other falling object  n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Employees were loading a scissor lift onto a rollback truck when the scissor lift turned and slid down the rollback truck. The scissor lift fell over sideways and it's guardrails struck an employee, fracturing their hip. The employee was hospitalized and required surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was working product in the back of his truck to prepare for a delivery when he tripped over a pallet and fell to the floor of his trailer. As he fell, he struck his head on the side wall of the trailer, resulting in head/neck injuries.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was stepping off a step ladder when they fell to the floor, resulting in an injury to the lumbar region of the back that required surgery. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level  less than 6 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee stepped off a platform onto a lower step, lost their footing, and fell to the floor approximately 17 inches below the platform. As a result, the employee fractured their left wrist and sprained their left ankle.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Two employees were placing items on a mezzanine. One employee fell from the mezzanine to the floor and suffered injuries to the head, neck, shoulder, and face.

sampleRecalls.title: Formation Inc. Recalls Coca-Cola® Themed Drinking Glasses Due to Laceration Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves clear double-walled soda glasses sold in a bell shape. The drinking glasses were sold in two sizes: 16 ounces and 22 ounces, and have various Coca-Cola® graphics printed on the glass.
hazards: The inner wall of the double-walled glasses can break easily, posing a laceration hazard to consumers.
injuries: Formation has received one report of a glass breaking during normal use. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Coca-Cola® ElectroPlasma Lamps Sold at Rite Aid Recalled for Burn Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: These lamps are in the shape of a Coca-Cola bottle. They are about 13.5 inches high. The inside of ElectroPlasma lamps simulate lightning bolts. A label on the bottom of the black base of the lamp reads: "Model No: 23-103," and "Lipan Industrial Co., Ltd."
hazards: There is a burn hazard caused by an electric arc between the metal bottle cap and a human contact point. An electric burn is possible if the distance between t…
injuries: The firm has received reports of two incidents involving an electrical arc to the finger, including one report of a minor burn.
